Interface ReactiveZSetOperations<K,V>

@NullUnmarked public interface ReactiveZSetOperations<K,V>
Reactive Redis operations for Sorted (ZSet) Commands.

Streams of methods returning Mono<K> or Flux<M> are terminated with InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException when RedisElementReader.read(ByteBuffer) returns null for a particular element as Reactive Streams prohibit the usage of null values.

Since:
2.0
Author:
Mark Paluch, Christoph Strobl, Andrey Shlykov
See Also:
  • Redis Documentation: Sorted Set Commands

    Method
    Description
    Mono<Boolean>
    add(@NonNull K key, @NonNull V value, double score)
    Add value to a sorted set at key, or update its score if it already exists.
    Mono<Long>

    Add tuples to a sorted set at key, or update their score if it already exists.
    Mono<Long>
    count(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Count number of elements within sorted set with scores between min and max.
    Mono<Boolean>
    delete(@NonNull K key)
    Removes the given key.
    default Flux<V>
    difference(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey)
    Diff sorted sets.

    difference(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys)
    Diff sorted sets.
    default Mono<Long>

    Diff sorted sets and store result in destination destKey.

    Diff sorted sets and store result in destination destKey.

    Diff sorted sets.

    Get count distinct random elements from set at key.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    distinctRandomMembersWithScore(@NonNull K key, long count)
    Get count distinct random elements with their score from set at key.

    Increment the score of element with value in sorted set by increment.

    Intersect sorted sets.

    Intersect sorted sets.

    intersectAndStore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey, @NonNull K destKey)
    Intersect sorted sets at key and otherKey and store result in destination destKey.

    intersectAndStore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys, @NonNull K destKey)
    Intersect sorted sets at key and otherKeys and store result in destination destKey.

    intersectAndStore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys, @NonNull K destKey, @NonNull Aggregate aggregate)
    Intersect sorted sets at key and otherKeys and store result in destination destKey.

    Intersect sorted sets at key and otherKeys and store result in destination destKey.

    intersectWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey)
    Intersect sorted sets.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    intersectWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys)
    Intersect sorted sets.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    intersectWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys, @NonNull Aggregate aggregate, @NonNull Weights weights)
    Intersect sorted sets.
    default Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    intersectWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<K> otherKeys, @NonNull Aggregate aggregate)
    Intersect sorted sets at key and otherKeys .
    Mono<Long>
    lexCount(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range)
    Count number of elements within sorted set with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound() applying lexicographical ordering.
    Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    popMax(@NonNull K key)
    Remove and return the value with its score having the highest score from sorted set at key.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    popMax(@NonNull K key, long count)
    Remove and return count values with their score having the highest score from sorted set at key.
    Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    popMax(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Duration timeout)
    Remove and return the value with its score having the highest score from sorted set at key.
    Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    popMin(@NonNull K key)
    Remove and return the value with its score having the lowest score from sorted set at key.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    popMin(@NonNull K key, long count)
    Remove and return count values with their score having the lowest score from sorted set at key.
    Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    popMin(@NonNull K key, Duration timeout)
    Remove and return the value with its score having the lowest score from sorted set at key.
    Mono<V>
    randomMember(@NonNull K key)
    Get random element from set at key.
    Flux<V>
    randomMembers(@NonNull K key, long count)
    Get count random elements from set at key.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    randomMembersWithScore(@NonNull K key, long count)
    Get count random elements with their score from set at key.
    Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    randomMemberWithScore(@NonNull K key)
    Get random element with its score from set at key.
    Flux<V>
    range(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Long> range)
    Get elements between start and end from sorted set.
    default Mono<Long>
    rangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range)
    Store all elements at dstKey with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Mono<Long>
    rangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    default @Nullable Mono<Long>
    rangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Store all elements at dstKey with ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Mono<Long>
    rangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Flux<V>
    rangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range)
    Get all elements with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Flux<V>
    rangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Get all elements n elements, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Flux<V>
    rangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Get elements where score is between min and max from sorted set.
    Flux<V>
    rangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Get elements in range from start to end where score is between min and max from sorted set.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    rangeByScoreWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Get set of Tuples where score is between min and max from sorted set.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    rangeByScoreWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Get set of Tuples in range from start to end where score is between min and max from sorted set.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    rangeWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Long> range)
    Get set of Tuples between start and end from sorted set.

    rank(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Object o)
    Determine the index of element with value in a sorted set.

    Remove elements in range from sorted set with key.

    removeRangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Remove elements with scores between min and max from sorted set with key.
    Flux<V>
    reverseRange(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Long> range)
    Get elements in range from start to end from sorted set ordered from high to low.
    default Mono<Long>
    reverseRangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range)
    Store all elements at dstKey with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Mono<Long>
    reverseRangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    default Mono<Long>
    reverseRangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Store all elements at dstKey with reverse ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Mono<Long>
    reverseRangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with reverse ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Flux<V>
    reverseRangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range)
    Get all elements with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Flux<V>
    reverseRangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Get all elements n elements, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
    Flux<V>
    reverseRangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Get elements where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered from high to low.
    Flux<V>
    reverseRangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Get elements in range from start to end where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered high -> low.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    reverseRangeByScoreWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
    Get set of Tuple where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered from high to low.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    reverseRangeByScoreWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
    Get set of Tuple in range from start to end where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered high -> low.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    reverseRangeWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Long> range)
    Get set of Tuples in range from start to end from sorted set ordered from high to low.
    Mono<Long>
    reverseRank(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Object o)
    Determine the index of element with value in a sorted set when scored high to low.
    default Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    scan(@NonNull K key)
    Use a Flux to iterate over entries in the sorted set at key.
    Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>>
    scan(@NonNull K key, @Nullable ScanOptions options)
    Use a Flux to iterate over entries in the sorted set at key given ScanOptions.
    Mono<Double>
    score(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Object o)
    Get the score of element with value from sorted set with key key.
    Mono<List<Double>>
    score(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Object @NonNull ... o)
    Get the scores of elements with values from sorted set with key key.
    Mono<Long>
    size(@NonNull K key)
    Returns the number of elements of the sorted set stored with given key.

    Union sorted sets.

  • Method Details

    • add

      Mono<Boolean> add(@NonNull K key, @NonNull V value, double score)
      Add value to a sorted set at key, or update its score if it already exists.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - the value.
      score - the score.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZADD

    • addAll

      Mono<Long> addAll(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<? extends @NonNull ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> tuples)
      Add tuples to a sorted set at key, or update their score if it already exists.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      tuples - the score.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZADD

    • remove

      Mono<Long> remove(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Object @NonNull ... values)
      Remove values from sorted set. Return number of removed elements.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      values - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREM

    • incrementScore

      Mono<Double> incrementScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull V value, double delta)
      Increment the score of element with value in sorted set by increment.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - the value.
      delta - the delta to add. Can be negative.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZINCRBY

      count - number of members to return.

      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if count is negative.

    • reverseRangeWithScores

      Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> reverseRangeWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Long> range)
      Get set of Tuples in range from start to end from sorted set ordered from high to low.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREVRANGE

    • reverseRangeByScore

      Flux<V> reverseRangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
      Get elements where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered from high to low.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE

    • reverseRangeByScoreWithScores

      Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> reverseRangeByScoreWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
      Get set of Tuple where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered from high to low.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE

    • reverseRangeByScore

      Flux<V> reverseRangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Get elements in range from start to end where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered high -> low.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      limit -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE

    • reverseRangeByScoreWithScores

      Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> reverseRangeByScoreWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Get set of Tuple in range from start to end where score is between min and max from sorted set ordered high -> low.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      limit -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE

    • rangeAndStoreByLex

      default Mono<Long> rangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range)
      Store all elements at dstKey with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • rangeAndStoreByLex

      Mono<Long> rangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      limit - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • reverseRangeAndStoreByLex

      default Mono<Long> reverseRangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range)
      Store all elements at dstKey with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • reverseRangeAndStoreByLex

      Mono<Long> reverseRangeAndStoreByLex(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at srcKey with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      limit - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • reverseRangeByLex(Object, Range, Limit)
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • rangeAndStoreByScore

      default @Nullable Mono<Long> rangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
      Store all elements at dstKey with ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • rangeAndStoreByScore

      Mono<Long> rangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      limit - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • reverseRangeAndStoreByScore

      default Mono<Long> reverseRangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
      Store all elements at dstKey with reverse ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • reverseRangeAndStoreByScore

      Mono<Long> reverseRangeAndStoreByScore(@NonNull K srcKey, @NonNull K dstKey, @NonNull Range<Double> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Store n elements at dstKey, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with reverse ordering by score from ZSET at srcKey with a score between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      limit - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the number of stored elements.
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGESTORE

    • scan

      default Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> scan(@NonNull K key)
      Use a Flux to iterate over entries in the sorted set at key. The resulting Flux acts as a cursor and issues ZSCAN commands itself as long as the subscriber signals demand.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the Flux emitting the values one by one or an empty Flux if none exist.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - when given key is null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZSCAN

    • scan

      Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> scan(@NonNull K key, @Nullable ScanOptions options)
      Use a Flux to iterate over entries in the sorted set at key given ScanOptions. The resulting Flux acts as a cursor and issues ZSCAN commands itself as long as the subscriber signals demand.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      options - must not be null. Use ScanOptions.NONE instead.
      Returns:
      the Flux emitting the values one by one or an empty Flux if none exist.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - when one of the required arguments is null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZSCAN

    • count

      Mono<Long> count(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
      Count number of elements within sorted set with scores between min and max.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZCOUNT

    • lexCount

      Mono<Long> lexCount(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range)
      Count number of elements within sorted set with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound() applying lexicographical ordering.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range - must not be null
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.4
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZLEXCOUNT

    • popMin

      Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> popMin(@NonNull K key)
      Remove and return the value with its score having the lowest score from sorted set at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZPOPMIN

    • popMin

      Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> popMin(@NonNull K key, long count)
      Remove and return count values with their score having the lowest score from sorted set at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      count - number of elements to pop.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZPOPMIN

    • popMin

      Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> popMin(@NonNull K key, Duration timeout)
      Remove and return the value with its score having the lowest score from sorted set at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      timeout - maximal duration to wait until an entry in the list at key is available. Must be either Duration.ZERO or greater 1 second, must not be null. A timeout of zero can be used to wait indefinitely. Durations between zero and one second are not supported.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZPOPMIN

    • popMax

      Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> popMax(@NonNull K key)
      Remove and return the value with its score having the highest score from sorted set at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZPOPMAX

    • popMax

      Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> popMax(@NonNull K key, long count)
      Remove and return count values with their score having the highest score from sorted set at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      count - number of elements to pop.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZPOPMAX

    • popMax

      Mono<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> popMax(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Duration timeout)
      Remove and return the value with its score having the highest score from sorted set at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      timeout - maximal duration to wait until an entry in the list at key is available. Must be either Duration.ZERO or greater 1 second, must not be null. A timeout of zero can be used to wait indefinitely. Durations between zero and one second are not supported.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZPOPMIN

    • size

      Mono<Long> size(@NonNull K key)
      Returns the number of elements of the sorted set stored with given key.
      Parameters:
      key -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZCARD

    • score

      Mono<Double> score(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Object o)
      Get the score of element with value from sorted set with key key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      o - the value.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZSCORE

    • score

      Mono<List<Double>> score(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Object @NonNull ... o)
      Get the scores of elements with values from sorted set with key key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      o - the values.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZMSCORE

    • removeRange

      Mono<Long> removeRange(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Long> range)
      Remove elements in range between start and end from sorted set with key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREMRANGEBYRANK

    • removeRangeByLex

      Mono<Long> removeRangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range)
      Remove elements in range from sorted set with key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      Returns:
      a Mono emitting the number or removed elements.
      Since:
      2.5
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREMRANGEBYRANK

    • removeRangeByScore

      Mono<Long> removeRangeByScore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<Double> range)
      Remove elements with scores between min and max from sorted set with key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREMRANGEBYSCORE

    • difference

      default Flux<V> difference(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey)
      Diff sorted sets.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZDIFF

    • difference

      Flux<V> difference(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys)

      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKeys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZDIFF

      default Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> differenceWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey)

      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZDIFF

    • differenceWithScores

      Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> differenceWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys)

      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKeys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6

      • Redis Documentation: ZDIFF

    • differenceAndStore

      default Mono<Long> differenceAndStore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey, @NonNull K destKey)
      Diff sorted sets and store result in destination destKey.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKey - must not be null.
      destKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZDIFFSTORE

    • differenceAndStore

      Mono<Long> differenceAndStore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<@NonNull K> otherKeys, @NonNull K destKey)
      Diff sorted sets and store result in destination destKey.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKeys - must not be null.
      destKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZDIFFSTORE

    • intersect

      default Flux<V> intersect(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey)

      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKey - must not be null.

      Since:

    • intersectWithScores

      default Flux<ZSetOperations.TypedTuple<V>> intersectWithScores(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Collection<K> otherKeys, @NonNull Aggregate aggregate)

      otherKeys - must not be null.

      2.6

      default Mono<Long> intersectAndStore(@NonNull K key, @NonNull K otherKey, @NonNull K destKey)

      Union sorted sets at key and otherKeys and store result in destination destKey.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      otherKeys - must not be null.

      weights - must not be null.
      Returns:

      2.1

    • rangeByLex

      Flux<V> rangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range)
      Get all elements with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGEBYLEX

    • rangeByLex

      Flux<V> rangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Get all elements n elements, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null
      range - must not be null.
      limit - can be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZRANGEBYLEX

    • reverseRangeByLex

      Flux<V> reverseRangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range)
      Get all elements with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      range - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREVRANGEBYLEX

    • reverseRangeByLex

      Flux<V> reverseRangeByLex(@NonNull K key, @NonNull Range<String> range, @NonNull Limit limit)
      Get all elements n elements, where n = Limit.getCount(), starting at Limit.getOffset() with reverse lexicographical ordering from ZSET at key with a value between Range.getLowerBound() and Range.getUpperBound().
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null
      range - must not be null.
      limit - can be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: ZREVRANGEBYLEX

    • delete

      Mono<Boolean> delete(@NonNull K key)
      Removes the given key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
